Consensus implied ~$925.94B (range $898.16–$953.72B) based on prior quarter $762.87B + trailing growth of +21.4% Q/Q.

Material risks (from 10-K Item 1A): In addition to competitors who manufacture products similar to those we produce, there are other technologies and products available that may compete with our technologies and products.
RISK FACTORS The following are certain risk factors that could affect our business, results of operations, financial condition or cash flows.

These risk factors should be considered along with any forward-looking statements contained in this Annual Report on Form 10-K, because these factors could cause our actual results or financial condition to differ materially from those projected in forward-looking statements.
The following discussion is not an all-inclusive listing of risks, although we believe these are the material risks that we face.
If any of the following occur, our business, results of operations, financial position, or cash flows could be adversely affected.
Risks Related to Our Business, Operations and Industry Our competitive position depends on our ability to develop new products and processes and may require significant investment.
The introduction by our competitors of products or processes using new developments that are better or lower cost than ours could render our products or processes obsolete or unmarketable.
The failure to do so could have a material adverse effect on our ability to grow our business and maintain our competitive position and on our results of operations and/or financial condition.

short_term.title: Short-term view (0–30 days · next earnings cycle)
bullets: Run-rate $1805.64B in FY2026 Q1, +101.2% YoY — accelerating trajectory entering the next print.

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ days · multi-year setup)
bullets: TTM revenue $5970B — in cyclical drawdown.
Sell-side: FY1 $6B → FY2 $7B (+12.7% multi-year top-line implied).
Forward EBIT margin: 28.0% — structural premium business over the long-cycle view.
6-quarter run-rate CAGR: +99.2% — the secular trajectory the market is pricing.
Sector thesis (Information Technology): Multi-year AI/cloud capex cycle remains the dominant secular theme.
long_term.takeaway: Long-cycle thesis is intact if the next 2–4 quarters confirm a stabilization in trajectory.

short_term.title: Short-term view (0–45 DTE · front-month tactical)
bullets: Front-month ATM IV: 143.3% — extreme (binary-event hedging implied).
Put/Call OI: 1.69 — put-heavy hedging (institutions defensive).
30-day implied move: ±21.3% ($297.31 – $457.83) — the 1-σ range the options market is pricing.
ATM skew: -0.95pp — balanced skew.

short_term.strategy: Sell 30–45 DTE cash-secured put at $359 (5% OTM). High IV (143%) + uptrend → collect premium with a willingness to be assigned at a discount. Roll if breached, otherwise let theta decay.

long_term.strategy: Sell 6-month strangle: short $340 put / short $415 call. Range-bound + high IV → premium-selling on both sides; profits if spot stays within ±10%; manage with rolls or assignment.
